items.title: GNB training and prediction flow
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  trainNode["labeled data (x, y)"]
  estNode["estimate P(y), mu_kj, sigma_sq_kj per class"]
  queryNode["new feature vector x"]
  scoreNode["log score_k = log P(y=k) + sum_j log N(x_j|mu, sigma_sq)"]
  predNode["predict argmax_k"]
  trainNode --> estNode
  estNode --> scoreNode
  queryNode --> scoreNode
  scoreNode --> predNode
items.caption: Training is closed-form parameter counting; inference scores each class with log prior plus summed log Gaussian likelihoods.

items.title: Per-class per-feature Gaussians
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  classANode["class A"]
  classBNode["class B"]
  feat1ANode["feature 1 ~ N(mu_A1, var_A1)"]
  feat2ANode["feature 2 ~ N(mu_A2, var_A2)"]
  feat1BNode["feature 1 ~ N(mu_B1, var_B1)"]
  feat2BNode["feature 2 ~ N(mu_B2, var_B2)"]
  productNode["P(x|y) ~ product of per-feature PDFs"]
  classANode --> feat1ANode
  classANode --> feat2ANode
  classBNode --> feat1BNode
  classBNode --> feat2BNode
  feat1ANode --> productNode
  feat2ANode --> productNode
  feat1BNode --> productNode
  feat2BNode --> productNode
items.caption: Each class owns a separate Gaussian per continuous feature; the naive assumption multiplies (or sums logs of) these likelihoods.

items.title: Generative vs discriminative placement
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  bayesNode["Bayes rule: P(y|x) proportional to P(y) P(x|y)"]
  gnbNode["GNB: Gaussian P(x|y) with naive product"]
  discNode["Logistic regression: model P(y|x) directly"]
  bayesNode --> gnbNode
  bayesNode --> discNode
items.caption: GNB is generative — it builds P(x|y) from Gaussian likelihoods then applies Bayes; logistic regression skips modeling P(x|y).
